commit.message:
Add in-world WebXR UI panel (prev/next/slider) (#1)

* add Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion simulation (3D)

New simulation mode: Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion on a 3D volume,
rendered via fullscreen raymarch of an isosurface on the v-field.
Introduces the first texture-backed sim state (rg16float 3D ping-pong)
and the first raymarched renderer in the codebase — plumbing that
follow-up sims (Physarum, wave equation) can reuse.

Presets: Spots, Mazes, Worms, Mitosis, Coral.

* Add in-world WebXR UI panel (prev/next/slider)

Renders a floating 3D panel in immersive-vr mode so users can switch
simulation modes and tweak one primary parameter without leaving the
headset.

- New procedural SDF shader (src/shaders/xr.ui.wgsl) draws a rounded
  background, two chevron buttons, and a slider track/fill/knob in one
  quad — no textures, no glyph atlas.
- Per-mode slider bindings in main.ts map to an existing state field
  (e.g. fluid.forceStrength, physics.G).
- Ray-plane hit-testing routes pinch input between UI and simulation
  through a single-enforcer check in updateXRSimulationInteraction.
- selectstart defers resolution to the first XRFrame so hit-tests have
  a real pose; selectend triggers button actions or cleanly clears.
- UI renders into the HDR scene between sim.render and bloom so it
  picks up tonemap + glow.

* fix reaction sim black-screen and add per-frame error surfacing

Two bugs in the initial reaction-diffusion implementation:

1. rg16float is NOT in WebGPU's baseline storage-texture format list.
   Texture creation failed silently and the sim rendered garbage or
   nothing at all. Switched to rgba16float (always in the baseline),
   using only .rg channels in the shader. Fixed bytesPerRow (N*4 → N*8)
   and seed array stride (*2 → *4) accordingly.

2. Sim failures produced zero user feedback — black screen or freeze,
   with no way to know what went wrong. Now:

   - ensureSimulation() wraps factory creation in validation/internal/
     OOM error scopes. Any failure surfaces via showSimError() into the
     existing top-left red overlay.

   - frame() wraps sim.compute/render in a frame-level validation scope
     plus a try/catch. On any failure the broken sim is destroyed and
     dropped from the registry so the app stops hammering bad GPU state
     every frame. The user sees the error and can switch modes.

This addresses the "black screen / freeze / eject from XR" experience —
whatever goes wrong next will say so.

* reaction sim: fix diffusion scaling + raymarch aliasing

Two linked bugs produced the "frozen boxes with ribs" look and the
"changes when I move the camera" artifact:

Physics:
- The Laplacian divided neighbors by 6, but the canonical Gray-Scott
  atlas (Du=0.2097, Dv=0.105) assumes a UNIT-weight Laplacian. Effective
  diffusion was running at 1/6 strength, so most presets froze as the
  reaction term couldn't compete with the weak spread. Removed /6.
- dt was 1.0, which is right at the stability edge for Du=0.2097
  (limit ~1/(6·Du) ≈ 0.79). Lowered to 0.7 for headroom.

Rendering:
- stepCount=96 through a 3-unit volume meant step size ≈ texel size.
  Under camera orbit, the fixed-stride march aligned to different texel
  boundaries per frame, producing visible ribs that appeared to "change
  with the camera". Bumped to 256 and added a per-pixel hash-based
  start offset (stochastic sampling), which converts what would be
  coherent aliasing into smooth noise that bloom easily absorbs.
- Widened the soft-iso smoothstep band from ±0.04 to ±0.08 so sub-texel
  surfaces fade in rather than popping.

All five presets should now evolve recognizably within a few seconds:
Spots stabilizes into blobs, Mazes into maze patterns, Mitosis visibly
divides, Coral fills space, Worms produces worm-like channels.

* XR UI panel: text labels, ray reticle, fix arrow direction

- Added text labels (PREV / NEXT / current slider parameter) via a
  canvas-rasterized label atlas uploaded to a GPUTexture. Labels are
  redrawn on mode change with dynamic font sizing so long labels like
  "GRAVITY" auto-fit.
- Added a glowing reticle at the XR ray / panel intersection whenever
  a pinch is active, giving Vision Pro users visual feedback about
  where their gaze-then-hand-corrected ray is pointing (since natural
  input only exposes a ray during a pinch).
- Fixed chevron direction: prev button now points left (<) and next
  points right (>).
- Widened button boxes to 0.36 world units so each fits a chevron
  plus a 2:1 aspect label rect with breathing room.
- Non-uniform label atlas sub-rects (2:1 for buttons, 4:1 for slider
  label) so text isn't stretched when sampled at the panel surface.
- Single-enforcer mode-cycling refactor: introduced selectMode() used
  by both DOM tab clicks and XR prev/next so state.mode, DOM classes,
  and simulation registry move together.
- XR slider drags now call syncUIFromState() + saveState() on release
  so DOM sliders show the updated value when the user exits VR.

* reaction sim: remove visible cube, scatter-noise seed

Three user-visible issues addressed:

1. Invisible boundary cube: patterns were piling up against the hard
   clamped-coord texture edge and you could see the cube outline.
   Added Dirichlet boundary in the compute shader — a smooth band at
   r > 0.80 of half-size pulls every cell toward (u=1, v=0), creating
   a "reservoir" zone that absorbs patterns before they reach the wall.
   Patterns now die in the interior; they literally cannot escape.

2. Visible cube edges in render: added a spherical alpha falloff
   (1 - smoothstep(0.78, 0.95, radial distance)) so the render has no
   cube edges at all — only a soft spherical fade to invisible.

3. "Keeps growing the same blobs again" on reset: the old seed was 12
   big spherical blobs in a fixed central region, so every reset
   produced the same general silhouette regardless of preset. Replaced
   with a scattered noise seed — 80 tiny random points (1-2 cell
   radius) in the center 40% of the volume. Each reset now looks
   genuinely different and early-time patterns are fine-grained
   instead of obviously spherical.

The reset plumbing itself was already correct: applyPreset calls
resetCurrentSim which destroys + recreates the sim, so the factory
re-runs Math.random for every preset click.

* fix reaction time knob, bg race, nbody classic reserved word

1. Reaction time slider: timeScale now drives substep count, not dt.
   Previously multiplying into dt blew past the explicit-Euler stability
   bound (~0.79 for Du=0.2097), producing NaN and a disappearing sim.

2. Reaction render ping-pong was off-by-one: renderBGs[pong] was reading
   the STALE texture. Swapped mapping so render sees the latest write.

3. Error scope handlers were deleting whatever sim currently lived at
   simulations[mode], which could be a fresh one the user just reset.
   Now captures the sim instance and only drops it if still the same.

4. nbody.classic.render.wgsl: renamed struct field 'active' -> 'enabled'.
   'active' is a WGSL reserved keyword, caused compile failure at 26:26.

* XR UI panel: add grab handle for repositioning

Adds a visionOS-style grab bar at the bottom of the panel that lets
users pinch-drag to reposition the panel anywhere in 3D space.

- New 'grab' element type in hit-testing and hover encoding
- Thin rounded pill at y=-0.40 below the slider, lights up with
  camera.accent on hover
- Drag tracks the world-space delta between initial and current
  ray/Z-plane intersection, offsetting XR_UI_PANEL_CENTER each frame
- Panel position persists across pinch releases within a session
- Grab state cleanly cleared on selectend alongside other UI state

* Update src/main.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update src/main.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* persistence: derive saved modes from DEFAULTS registry

saveState/loadState previously hardcoded each sim mode by name, causing
reaction and physics_classic to be silently omitted. Rather than adding
to the manual list, loop over Object.keys(DEFAULTS) — the canonical mode
registry — so any future mode added to DEFAULTS is automatically persisted
with no further edits.

[LAW:one-source-of-truth] DEFAULTS was already the single source of which
modes exist; the persistence layer now derives from it instead of
maintaining a parallel list.

* mobile UI: bottom sheet controls, touch gestures, mode stepper

Auto-detects mobile via matchMedia (max-width: 768px) and switches to
a mobile-optimized layout:

- Canvas fills full viewport, controls become a bottom sheet overlay
  with drag handle and peek state showing the mode stepper
- Prev/next mode stepper replaces the 6-tab bar on small screens
- FAB buttons (pause/reset/controls) overlay the canvas top-right
- Multi-touch gesture system: 1-finger = sim interaction, 2-finger
  drag = orbit camera, pinch = zoom (replaces ctrl+drag and wheel)
- Touch-friendly sizing: 24px slider thumbs, 44px buttons
- Reduced default particle counts for mobile GPUs on fresh installs
- Theme selector and prompt bar hidden on mobile
- Shader editor goes full-width on mobile
- Desktop UI completely unchanged

fix: Resolve infinite token refresh loop on missing scope 401s (#257)

* Initial plan

* Fix infinite token refresh loop when scope is missing

When a 401 response is due to missing scopes (e.g., "Missing scope:
moderator:read:followers"), the token refresh would not resolve the
issue since refreshing preserves the same scopes. Previously, this
caused an infinite loop of refresh-retry cycles.

Changes:
- Parse the 401 error response body before attempting token refresh
- Skip token refresh when error message indicates missing scopes
- Limit token refresh to one attempt per request to prevent infinite
  loops for any persistent 401 error
- Add tests for both missing scope and persistent 401 scenarios

commit.message:
fix: wait for EOV replenishment before advancing RADIO queues

RADIO/wave queues have very short playable_lists (2 items). Advancing
the index past the list length causes Ynison error 400030001
'Current playable index out of playable list range' and disconnection.

Now _signal_track_completion checks if next_index is within bounds:
- Within bounds: advance immediately (playlists with many tracks)
- At end: send EOV first, wait up to 5s for queue to grow, then advance

_handle_ynison_state signals the asyncio.Event when the replenished
queue arrives, enabling the advance to proceed.

commit.message:
fix(tasks): re-validate SSRF redirect targets against network:private grant scope (#407)

* fix(tasks): re-validate SSRF redirect targets against network:private grant scope

FetchUrlTask declares its network:private requirement scoped to the input
URL's origin, but safeFetch only received a boolean allowPrivate flag — so
once a task was authorized for one private host, a compromised upstream
could redirect across private hosts/ports via Location headers without
ever failing the scope check the enforcer evaluated at preflight.

Thread the declared scope (via the existing urlResourcePattern) through
SafeFetchOptions.privateResourceScopes and re-enforce it on every redirect
hop in both the browser and server SafeFetch implementations. Least
privilege: the task can only reach the private origin it was specifically
authorized for, matching what the task-graph enforcer approved.

https://claude.ai/code/session_0134L6Tzad2N6k4nXeWGPbY7

* Fix error messages and trailing-dot normalization in urlMatchesScope

commit.message:
Feat/cli restore (#6)

* Add Claude Code guard hooks and install-all CLI

Add CLI commands for installing/uninstalling Claude Code guard hooks (install-hooks, uninstall-hooks, and aliases) and a convenience command (install-all / restore) to reinstall statusline, MCP and hooks. Implement getGuardHooks(), installHooks() and uninstallHooks() in installer.ts which inject/remove a hooks section into the Claude Code settings file (pointing to scripts under ~/Sriinnu/Personal/script-helpers). installHooks checks current settings to avoid redundant writes, writes the hooks JSON, and prints a summary; uninstallHooks removes the hooks key. installAll runs installStatusline, installMCP and installHooks and prompts to restart editors.

* feat: add project cleanup, restore, themes, and powerline statusline

- CleanupService with preview/execute/backup/restore across all 16 providers
- Per-provider cleaners: simple-file (9), task-dir (2), claude-code (7 paths),
  sqlite (2), openclaw (files+index)
- CLI: interactive `tokmeter cleanup` with multi-select + type DELETE confirm
- CLI: `tokmeter restore` with backup listing and RESTORE confirm
- TUI: tab 5 (Cleanup) with browse/preview/confirm/done phases
- MCP: 4 new tools (cleanup_preview, cleanup_execute, backups, restore)
- Daemon: HTTP REST API on port+1 for future macOS/Windows native apps
- Theme system: 6 built-in themes loaded from ~/.config/tokmeter/config.json
- Statusline: powerline segments with Nerd Font glyphs (󰍰 agent, 󰳞 folder,
   git, 󰍛 context), theme-colored backgrounds
- Security: tar --no-absolute-names, backup_id sanitization, localhost-only CORS,
  body size limits, backup failure aborts deletion
- Installer: local-first CLI resolution (bun) with npx fallback

* Enable builds for Biome and esbuild

Add pnpm-workspace.yaml with allowBuilds entries for '@biomejs/biome' and 'esbuild' so pnpm will permit running build scripts for these workspace packages. This ensures workspace build commands can execute for those packages.

* fix: security hardening, twilight statusline, and installer reliability

Security:
- Daemon HTTP API now requires bearer token auth (0600 file)
- Tar restore validates archive contents for ../ path traversal
- Tar backup uses --no-absolute-names for consistency
- Empty-archive ghost metadata no longer written
- Leaked save-state interval tracked and cleared on daemon stop
- httpServer error handler prevents crash on EADDRINUSE

Statusline:
- Twilight color palette: indigo → blue → teal → slate → amber
- ❯ chevron separators with flowing color transitions
- Animated Unicode icons (no Nerd Font required, opt-in via config)
- Adaptive text color flips dark/light based on bg luminance
- Nerd Font toggle: NERD_FONT=1 env or nerdFont: true in config

Installer:
- Uses node + dist/cli.js instead of bun + source (reliable in subprocesses)
- installHooks merges with existing hooks instead of overwriting
- Corrupt JSON config guard — refuses to modify malformed files
- Falls back to npx @sriinnu/drishti when not in source tree

* fix: SQLite backup, Zed config key, MCP tool naming, dead code cleanup

Critical:
- SQLite rows now exported to JSON dump before deletion, included in
  tar backup archive. Restore path recovers both files and SQL data.

High:
- Zed installer writes to context_servers (not mcpServers) via mcpKey
- uninstallMCP also respects per-editor mcpKey

Medium:
- Tools 17-20 renamed with drishti_ prefix for naming consistency
- All cleanup/restore MCP tools wrapped in try/catch with isError: true
- Confirm failures return isError: true per MCP protocol
- DRY: shared buildCleanupFilter() replaces duplicated filter construction

Low:
- Removed ~80 lines dead code from statusline (rainbow, animTokenFlow,
  animCost, animContextBar, animBurnRate, unused PARTICLES variants)

* fix: symlink safety, path validation, connect timeout, colorblind-safe theme

- Cleaners use lstat() instead of stat() to avoid following symlinks
- Archive path validation uses resolve() to catch symlinks and ../ in meta
- DaemonClient.connect() has 3s timeout (was missing, could hang forever)
- High-contrast theme uses blue/orange instead of red/green for CVD safety

* fix: GPT-5 pricing 8x off and cached tokens double-charged

Two pricing bugs combined to inflate Codex costs by ~24x:

1. Static table had gpt-5 at \$10/\$40 (those are gpt-4-turbo rates).
   Real OpenAI rates: gpt-5 \$1.25/\$10, gpt-5.2 \$1.75/\$14, gpt-5.4 \$2.50/\$15.
   Removed the wrong gpt-5 entry — kosha-discovery resolves the gpt-5.x
   family correctly via the OpenRouter mirror.

2. OpenAI reports input_tokens as TOTAL (uncached + cached), but the cost
   calculator assumed Anthropic semantics (input_tokens = uncached only).
   Result: every cached token was billed once at the full input rate
   (line 420) AND once at the cache rate (line 423). For Codex sessions
   where 95%+ of input volume is cached, this roughly 10x overcharged.

   Codex parser now subtracts cached_input_tokens from input_tokens before
   creating records, matching Anthropic semantics.

Plus a 10% cache-read fallback in calculateCost(): both OpenAI and
Anthropic price cached input at ~10% of full input. When the resolver
returns pricing without an explicit cacheReadPerMillion (e.g. kosha
results from OpenRouter), apply that ratio automatically.

Impact: April 8 Codex cost \$118,916 -> \$5,001 (23.8x reduction).

* feat: disney-pixel statusline with rounded pill caps + cache-write fallback

Statusline redesign (Disney pixel animation principles):
- Rounded pill caps via ◖/◗ half-circles — bar reads as a capsule shape,
  not a sharp rectangle. Nerd Font mode uses the patched powerline caps.
- Logo segment now has its own breathing background that cycles through
  five purple shades (indigo → deep violet → violet → bright violet → peak)
  with asymmetric timing — feels alive, not metronomic.
- Hero logo: ♾️ infinity with an orbiting star (✨ ⭐) that moves right →
  none → left → none across 8 frames, creating visible coordinated motion.
- Agent: 🧞 Genie (Disney wish-granter — you ask, AI delivers) with
  periodic sparkle. Width-locked to prevent segment jitter.
- Context: 🪟 Window (literal "context window" pun)
- Project: bare name on its colored segment — segment color IS the marker
- Today: bare word, no chart icon — less is more
- Adaptive text color: dark on bright bg, white on dark bg via luminance check
- All segment text rendered bold for glyph visibility across terminal fonts

Pricing:
- Cache writes fall back to 1.25× input rate when no explicit
  cacheWritePerMillion (matches Anthropic's premium for the request that
  creates the cache). Was returning $0 when resolver omitted the field.

* feat: TUI theme integration + macOS menubar app via SPM + HTTP API

TUI theme integration:
- New packages/tui/src/theme.ts adapter loads the user's theme from
  ~/.config/tokmeter/config.json and exposes a flat hex color map.
- Replaced hardcoded Ink color names ("cyan", "gray", "red", etc.) with
  theme references in index.tsx, CleanupView, OverviewView, ModelsView,
  DailyView, StatsView. The TUI now respects the active theme.
- Ink accepts hex strings directly, so the substitution is straightforward.

macOS menubar app:
- Restructured packages/macos-bar/ into Swift Package Manager layout.
  Single file split into: Models, DaemonClient, TokmeterLoader, View, App.
- New DaemonClient: HTTP REST client for the daemon API on localhost:9877.
  Uses URLSession with 5s timeout, parallel async fetches for stats/daily/models.
  PID-file check before requests, clear errors when daemon is offline.
- Replaced subprocess-spawning loader with URLSession-based fetcher that
  refreshes every 30s.
- Twilight-palette UI to match the statusline: violet header, amber chart
  gradients. Brand glyph 【♾️】 in the menu bar.
- bundle.sh: builds release binary, wraps in proper .app with Info.plist
  (LSUIElement=true so no Dock icon), ad-hoc signs for local Gatekeeper.
- README documents architecture, build, distribution.

Build: swift build -c release   →   ./bundle.sh   →   open TokmeterBar.app

* fix: full production-readiness sweep from 4-agent review

Crash bugs (would have shipped broken):
- TUI hooks-order violation: useMemo calls were after early returns,
  React threw "Rendered more hooks than during the previous render"
  the moment scan() resolved. Hoisted hooks above the early returns.
- TUI process handlers no longer hard-exit on unhandledRejection /
  uncaughtException — that left the terminal in alt-screen mode.
- statusline formatContextMini RangeError: pct >1 made (w-filled)
  negative, .repeat(-N) throws. Clamped pct to [0,1].

Pricing correctness (scope expansion of the Codex bug):
- Qwen parser had the same OpenAI-style double-count bug (Google's
  promptTokenCount is total including cached). Fixed.
- Gemini parser had the same bug (tokens.input is total). Fixed.
- Bumped parser cache version 1->2 so existing users get a clean
  re-scan after upgrade instead of serving stale cached records.
- Added explicit cacheReadPerMillion entries to all Gemini 2.5/2.0/1.5
  models (~25% of input, NOT the 10% fallback) and DeepSeek family
  (~26% of input). Prevents the universal 10% fallback from
  undercharging these providers.
- Gated 1.25x cache-write fallback to only fire when an explicit rate
  exists (matches Anthropic semantics; OpenAI/Gemini have no
  cache-write concept and would be wrongly charged otherwise).

Statusline performance + visual:
- /tmp/-cached git info per cwd (5s TTL) — was running execSync x2
  every 200ms, blocking 50-300ms per render on busy repos.
- /tmp/-cached "today" totals (60s TTL) — was full-disk-scanning every
  200ms when daemon was offline. Hundreds of MB of JSONL reads on the
  hot path. Now bounded to once per minute.
- Fixed daemon-connected-but-idle hitting the expensive scan fallback.
- Fixed logo column-shift: infinity now anchored at column 0 in all
  frames (was alternating between 1 and 0).
- Fixed emoji width jitter on the genie animation: both frames are
  width-stable (genie + 1-cell sparkle slot). No more horizontal
  shimmer that read as "the terminal is glitching".
- Removed chevron-in-pill visual contradiction: Unicode pill mode now
  uses adjacent colored cells with no separator glyphs inside the
  pill. Nerd Font mode keeps the classic powerline arrows.
- Truncated long project names to 24 chars + ellipsis so the bar
  doesn't wrap on 80-col terms.
- Luminance threshold raised from 0.45 (dead branch — never fired for
  any twilight palette color) to 0.5 (WCAG-aligned).
- Removed dead plSegment() function.
- Unified FALLBACK_STATUSLINE constant — was duplicated in cli.ts and
  formatter.ts and could drift.
- COLORTERM detection: fall back to 256-color if terminal doesn't
  advertise truecolor, instead of forcing level-3 unconditionally.

TUI theme integration completeness:
- Themed BarChart, Heatmap, Sparkline (the chart components were left
  hardcoded — switching themes had no effect on Daily/Models tabs).
- Added T.text and T.bg roles to the theme adapter.
- Fixed missed hardcoded "cyan" in CleanupView cursor highlight.
- Fixed ModelsView semantic role: was using T.input for the provider
  column (input is for input-token coloring); now uses T.secondary.

CleanupView state machine fixes:
- Reset cursor + scrollOffset after cleanup so we never point past the
  (likely smaller) post-cleanup project list.
- Fresh accumulator object in runPreview instead of mutating the first
  preview's returned object — CleanupService may cache or reuse it.
- Wrapped core.scan() reset with .catch() — was silently swallowing
  re-scan failures, leaving the UI on stale data forever.
- selectedProjects is now memoized (stable identity for useCallback).
- Added "case-sensitive" hint on the DELETE confirmation screen.
- Fixed useEffect deps for cursor scroll-into-view (was missing
  scrollOffset, theoretical stale closure).

macOS menubar app:
- Added reentrancy guard to TokmeterLoader.loadData() — prevents the
  user spam-clicking refresh from interleaving two concurrent fetches.
- Exponential-backoff retry (3 attempts: 0s, 0.5s, 1s) for transient
  daemon-not-ready errors after `drishti daemon start`.
- New hasFreshData flag — when daemon dies between refreshes, the
  popover dims the stale numbers and shows a "STALE" badge instead
  of just leaving the old numbers visible with no indication.
- Replaced .help() tooltip (undiscoverable) with a visible error
  banner below the header when offline.
- Added accessibilityLabel on the menu bar string and refresh button
  for VoiceOver: "Tokmeter: today's cost is $X.YY".
- Verified API contract end-to-end: StatsData/DailyData/ModelData
  shapes match getStats() / getDailyBreakdown() / getModelCosts().
- Note: the reviewer's claim about the wrong port (9877 vs 9878) was
  incorrect — DAEMON_PORT is 9876 and HTTP is +1 = 9877. Verified.

* feat: prod-ready sweep + Pixar-magazine typography innovation

Typography innovation (mixed-font statusline):
- New typography.ts with smallCaps() + italicMath() + boldMath() helpers.
  Uses Unicode Mathematical Alphanumeric Symbols (U+1D400+) and Latin
  Extended-D so any modern terminal font renders them — no font config.
- Project name now renders in small caps: tokmeter -> ᴛᴏᴋᴍᴇᴛᴇʀ. Quietly
  distinguished, not shouty. Magazine-style identification.
- "today" label renders in mathematical italic: today -> 𝑡𝑜𝑑𝑎𝑦. The italic
  implies "ephemeral / contextual" instead of permanent identifier.
- Pixar magazine rule: 2-3 typefaces max. We use small-caps + italic +
  default. Three weights working together to create visual hierarchy.

Statusline hot-path safety:
- Per-user cache dir at ${tmpdir}/drishti-${uid}/ with mode 0700, files
  written mode 0600. Multi-user systems no longer leak one user's totals
  to another via shared /tmp paths.
- Git info cache is now CONTENT-AWARE: keyed on .git/HEAD mtime, so a
  branch switch is reflected instantly with no polling delay. TTL is
  the safety net at 10s (was 5s). Single fs read on the hot path.
- Today totals cache keyed on YYYY-MM-DD: at midnight rollover the
  cache is invalidated automatically so yesterday's number doesn't
  linger as today's. 60s TTL is the secondary defense.
- Corrupt cache files are now deleted on next read instead of failing
  JSON.parse forever.

Statusline visual:
- Hairline ▏ separator between pill segments — gives boundary definition
  without contradicting the pill metaphor (no chevron teeth). 1-pixel
  vertical rule colored as the next segment's bg sitting on the current.

Color level detection:
- Reverted COLORTERM-based downgrade. Default is now level 3 (truecolor)
  unconditionally. Twilight palette is designed for truecolor and looks
  noticeably worse downsampled. Opt-outs: NO_COLOR, TERM=dumb, FORCE_COLOR.

macOS app — daemon API contract:
- Daemon now sends X-Drishti-API: drishti-api/1 header on every response.
- TokmeterBar checks the MAJOR version and surfaces a clear "incompatible
  daemon" error instead of failing with a confusing JSON decode crash.
- Bumping the daemon's API version forces native clients to reject
  responses they can't safely decode.
- Removed dead token-reading code (menubar app is GET-only, never POSTs).
- Daemon also sends hardening headers: X-Content-Type-Options, Cache-Control.
- bundle.sh: removed deprecated --deep flag from codesign (no nested
  frameworks; single .app sign is correct since Big Sur).

Tests (regression coverage for the bug surfaces that hid in the wild):
- pricing.test.ts: 6 new calculateCost tests
  * claude-opus-4-6 full math: $110.25 for 1M tokens of every kind
  * Cached input not double-charged: $2.925 vs the 24x bug
  * 10% cache-read fallback for gpt-4 (no explicit rate)
  * Cache-write fallback DOES NOT fire for non-Anthropic (correctly $0)
  * Unknown model returns 0 (no static, no kosha hit)
  * gemini-2.5-pro uses explicit 25% rate, not the 10% fallback
- codex.test.ts: 5 new parser tests with file-system fixtures
  * Subtracts cached_input_tokens from input_tokens (Anthropic semantics)
  * Computes deltas across consecutive cumulative events
  * Preserves original timestamp from JSONL
  * Pathological cache > input clamps to zero
  * Skips zero-token events
- typography.test.ts: 10 new tests for smallCaps/italicMath/boldMath

25 tests total, all passing. Anyone reintroducing the cached-token
double-count bug now hits a hard test failure on the diff.

* feat: full release pipeline (Sparkle + sign + notarize) + Pixar UI redesign

Sparkle 2.x auto-update integration:
- Added Sparkle SPM dependency (v2.6.0+, resolves to 2.9.1)
- New UpdaterController wraps SPUStandardUpdaterController with KVO-bound
  canCheckForUpdates published property (init true to avoid button flicker
  on first popover open). Combine import explicit instead of relying on
  SwiftUI transitive re-export.
- "Check for Updates..." button wired into the popover footer
- entitlements.plist for hardened runtime: network.client + cs.allow-jit +
  cs.disable-library-validation (required by Sparkle's installer XPC services)
- appcast.xml feed template
- generate-sparkle-keys.sh helper for EdDSA keypair (looks in both
  .build/artifacts/sparkle and .build/checkouts paths)

Release pipeline (bundle.sh) — three modes:
- ./bundle.sh             dev: ad-hoc signed, INSTALLED to /Applications by default
- ./bundle.sh --signed    Developer ID signed (no notarization)
- ./bundle.sh --release   signed + notarized + stapled + appcast generated
- Sparkle.framework signed in correct order: nested XPC services first,
  then framework, then main app — works in BOTH dev and release modes
- install_name_tool fixes the @executable_path/../Frameworks rpath
- Hard failures in --release mode for missing creds
- notarytool failures fetch the detailed log via xcrun notarytool log
- Python appcast updater uses xml.etree (whitespace-tolerant) and reads
  via os.environ instead of fragile heredoc interpolation
- App icon generated programmatically by Core Graphics (generate-icon.sh)
  twilight gradient squircle + infinity glyph + sparkle accent
- CFBundleIconFile / CFBundleIconName so Finder shows the icon

Daemon HTTP API performance:
- New /api/ready endpoint: tiny health check, never blocks on a scan
- New /api/quick endpoint: returns cached stats immediately, ready=false
  + zeros if warming. Lets the menubar render skeleton instead of timing out.
- New /api/sessions endpoint: 50 most recently used projects, sorted by
  lastUsed descending. Powers the scrollable session list.
- getHttpCore() coalesces concurrent callers — only one scan runs at a time.
- Daemon warms HTTP core asynchronously on startup so the first user
  request hits a hot cache.
- API version header X-Drishti-API on every response. Native clients
  verify major version and surface clear errors instead of decode crashes.

macOS app — Pixar/Disney UI redesign:
- Hero header with breathing twilight gradient (indigo -> violet -> amber)
  driven by autoreversing 4s animation. Animated infinity glyph.
- Skeleton shimmer state for ALL data points while warming. Never shows
  zeros — pulsing placeholders that fade into real numbers.
- WARMING / STALE pills in the header reflect daemon state.
- Stat cards with color-coded borders + numericText content transitions.
- Top 5 models with twilight->amber gradient bars.
- 7-day chart with gradient stroke + area fill, hidden y-axis.
- Sessions list: scrollable, up to 50 items, "Show all N" expand button
  past 8. Handles 10/20/50+ sessions.
- Footer with Refresh / Updates / Quit + accessibility labels.
- Two-phase loader: phase 1 hits /api/quick (instant), phase 2 fetches
  details in parallel with independent failure modes.
- URLSession timeout bumped 5s -> 60s for cold-daemon first request.

Tests:
- New qwen.test.ts: 4 tests pinning Google-API cached-token semantics fix.
- New gemini.test.ts: 4 tests for the Gemini CLI parser + session timestamp
  fallback path.

8 new parser tests, all passing. Total test suite now 33.

* fix: notch-widget hero shape + project basename in sessions list

- Hero header now uses UnevenRoundedRectangle with 0pt top corners and
  24pt bottom corners, flush with the popover edges. Visually "hangs"
  from the top of the window like a macOS notch / Dynamic Island widget.
  Drop shadow underneath sells the hanging depth.

- Sessions list shows project BASENAME only (tokmeter) instead of the
  full filesystem path (/Users/srinivaspendela/.../tokmeter). Hover the
  row to see the full path via the .help() tooltip.

- New projectBasename() helper handles both Unix (/) and Windows (\)
  separators and trims trailing whitespace/separators.

* fix: Codex dedup + OpenAI 50% cache rate + lint + CLI fallback + heartbeat

Data integrity:
- Fixed Codex duplicate records from last_token_usage fallback (~35K dupes,
  ~10% cost inflation). CACHE_VERSION 3 forces clean rescan.
- OpenAI cache rate is 50% not 10%. Added explicit cacheReadPerMillion to
  ALL GPT/O-series models (gpt-4o, gpt-4, o3, o4-mini, etc). The 10%
  universal fallback was undercharging OpenAI cached reads by 5x.

Lint:
- Biome auto-fixed 19 files. 59 tests passing, 0 errors.

macOS bar:
- CLI fallback when daemon offline (login shell subprocess for full PATH).
- Daemon heartbeat (green pulse / red dot) in footer.
- Footer pinned outside scroll, compact error banner, project basename.
- Notch-widget hero with UnevenRoundedRectangle.

* fix: 12-pass review sweep — all blockers + should-fixes resolved

BLOCKER B2 — SF Symbol menu bar:
- Replaced emoji ♾️ with Image(systemName: "infinity") SF Symbol.
  Renders correctly on light/dark menu bars per Apple HIG.

S1 — Width jitter:
- Statusline infinity animation uses ✦/✧ (both 1-cell) instead of
  mixed-width emoji ✨/⭐/🌟. Genie is now static (no more ·/✨ mix).

S2 — Sync I/O in view body:
- Added @Published isDaemonAlive to TokmeterLoader, updated on each
  loadData(). View reads the published property instead of calling
  DaemonClient.shared.isDaemonRunning (which does file I/O + kill()).

S6 — Heartbeat timing:
- Separate @State heartbeatPhase with 0.8s animation cycle. No longer
  shares the hero's 4s breathing phase. Feels like a real heartbeat.

S7/S8 — Chart theme colors:
- Heatmap: derives intensity ramp from T.accent with opacity steps
  instead of hardcoded GitHub greens.
- BarChart: T.cost (amber) instead of T.success (green) for cost data.
- Sparkline: T.accent instead of T.success.

S9 — NaN guard:
- roundPricing returns null if any pricing field is NaN or Infinity.
  Prevents malformed kosha data from producing NaN costs.

S10 — Claude Code dedup:
- Tracks lastUsageKey per source file. Consecutive assistant messages
  with identical input:output:cacheRead token counts are skipped.
  Eliminates the remaining ~719 phantom duplicates.

N1 — Context icon:
- Replaced 🪟 with empty string. The ▓▓░░░░░░ progress bar IS the
  context indicator — no extra glyph needed.

Test fix:
- Updated stale cache-read fallback test from gpt-4 (which now has
  explicit 50% cache rate) to grok-4 (which has none), preserving
  the 10% universal fallback test path.

* Add CLI helpers and history snapshot

Introduce programmatic CLI helpers and several core improvements.

- Add programmatic convenience API for @sriinnu/tokmeter-cli (loadTokmeterSummary, loadTokmeterProjects, loadTokmeterModels, loadTokmeterDailyBreakdown, loadTokmeterStats, lookupTokmeterPricing) and re-export runCleanup/runDigest/runRestore (packages/cli/src/index.ts).
- Add docs and README/SKILL updates describing how to consume Tokmeter from other apps (docs/consuming-tokmeter.md, README.md, SKILL.md).
- Implement frozen pre-today history snapshot storage with load/save/invalidate helpers (packages/core/src/history-snapshot.ts) and local calendar helpers (date-utils).
- Add project name normalization/matching utilities with tests (project-name.ts, project-name.test.ts) and update many parsers/aggregator to use canonicalized project names.
- Improve cleanup/digest/restore CLI flows (shortPath, consolidated preview/execute, safer return instead of process.exit, formatting tweaks) and strengthen cache invalidation (invalidateHistorySnapshot, invalidateSummaryCache) in cleanup-service.
- Enhance record cache handling and path utilities (parsers/utils.ts) and minor parser fixes/augmentations for OpenCode, Cursor, Codex, Kilo, Pi.
- Add editor config and gitattributes and adjust biome.json formatting/overrides.

These changes make embedding Tokmeter in other tools easier, add stable frozen history for faster dashboards, and normalize cross-platform project names for more accurate filtering.

* daemon: async CLI + background detach support

Make runDaemonCLI async and await it from the top-level CLI. Introduce an internal DAEMON_CHILD_FLAG so the process can either run the daemon in-foreground (when set) or spawn a detached background child using spawn() (avoiding fork() / ESM issues). Parent exits immediately after spawning the child; child unrefs and writes PID. Improve stop/restart behavior by waiting briefly for the daemon to exit/clean up (using Atomics.wait) and delegate restart to the start flow to ensure proper teardown before relaunch.

* fix: macOS bar CLI fallback uses stats --json (not full 150MB summary)

The CLI fallback was running tokmeter --json which dumps ALL records
(150MB+). This exceeded the 30s subprocess timeout every time, making
the menubar show "CLI scan failed" when the daemon was offline.

Fix: use tokmeter stats --json which returns just the aggregated
StatsData (~500 bytes) in a few seconds. The decoder tries full
TokmeterFullJSON first, falls back to StatsData-only gracefully.

Also increased timeout from 30s to 120s as a safety net for cold scans.

* feat: GitHub Actions macOS release + design system + hardened gitignore

GitHub Actions:
- release-macos.yml: builds, signs (Developer ID), notarizes (API key
  auth), staples, Sparkle-signs, uploads zip on release creation.
  Also supports manual workflow_dispatch with version input.
- Triggers alongside existing ci.yml (build+test) and publish.yml (npm).

Gitignore hardened:
- Global patterns for .env, *.p8, *.p12, *.pem, private keys
- Sparkle keys (sparkle_ed25519_priv, sparkle_private_key)
- .env.example explicitly NOT ignored (whitelisted with !)
- Verified: zero secrets in staging area

Web dashboard (11 files tokenized):
- All pages + charts use webTheme.spacing/radii/elevation/typography/
  motion tokens. Zero hardcoded magic numbers outside theme.ts.
- fadeUp entrance animations, hover-lift transitions on cards.
- Plotly charts use webTheme.charts.* for consistent colors.

macOS bar polish:
- Credits + version + settings gear + dark/light mode + model icon
- bundle.sh supports API key auth (Runic pattern) for notarization
- Tokmeter's own Sparkle keypair (separate from Runic)
- NSHumanReadableCopyright in Info.plist

Typography:

- Fraktur+Script available via sanskritTheme for flowing names

Add incremental PR scanning to reduce API quota usage (#55)

* Add incremental PR scanning to reduce API quota usage

Skip expensive GraphQL detail queries for PRs unchanged since the last scan.
Uses fingerprinting (updatedAt, labels, mergeable, draft, files) to detect
changes cheaply from PR list data. Carries forward cached triage results
for unchanged PRs, refreshing only those that actually changed.

Expected improvements:
- ~40-70% fewer GraphQL API calls per scan run, depending on repo churn
- Enables scanning more repos or more frequent refreshes within the same
  quota budget (currently ~200-500 calls/run across 12 repos)
- Priority repos (runtime, aspnetcore) scanned 4x/day should see the
  biggest savings since most PRs don't change between runs

Fail-safe design:
- Any incremental error falls back to full scan automatically
- Module import failure triggers full fallback shims (all 5 functions)
- Corrupt/missing previous scan.json disables incremental mode gracefully
- Cache version field forces full refresh when scoring logic changes
- 12h TTL bounds maximum staleness of carried-forward data
- Non-SUCCESS CI always forces refresh (catches reruns)
- GitHub Actions ::warning:: annotations surface fallback events

New files:
- scripts/IncrementalScan.psm1: Extracted module (testable, reusable)
- scripts/Tests/IncrementalScan.Tests.ps1: 31 Pester tests

Phase 1 of #54

fix(config): Fix reports.register() failing with "Cannot call TaskOutputs.file() after task started execution" (#1618) (#1648)

* Initial plan

* fix: resolve reports.register() failing with TaskOutputs.file() error during execution

When using reports.register() for lazy report registration, the domain objects
are realized at iteration time. Previously, the factory function called
(outputs as TaskOutputs).file() which fails if called during task execution.

Fix: remove the outputs.file() call from the factory and instead register all
required reports' output files via a lazy provider in the init block. The
provider is evaluated at task-graph finalization (before execution), not during
the task action itself.

Also adds a functional test covering the exact scenario from issue #1539:
registering multiple reports with register() without explicit required=true.

Fixes #1539



* fix: correct output-tracking for lazily-registered reports

The previous approach had two bugs caught by CI:

1. ArchUnit violation: outputs.files() without explicit cast caused Kotlin
   to infer AbstractTask.getOutputs() return type as TaskOutputsInternal
   (internal Gradle API). Fix: cast to org.gradle.api.tasks.TaskOutputs.

2. Lazy items excluded from output tracking: Kotlin's Collection.filter{}
   calls Iterable.iterator() which only returns already-realized items.
   Items registered with reports.register() stay pending/lazy and are
   invisible to iterator(), so the provider returned an empty file list.
   Fix: use reports.matching{} (Gradle's API) which realizes pending items
   when the resulting collection is iterated.

Also uses an anonymous Callable object instead of a lambda, following the
existing NamedDomainObjectFactory pattern for configuration-cache safety.

Fixes #1539

* Initial plan



* fix: pre-realize pending reports to prevent config-cache ConcurrentModificationException

When Gradle serializes the task state for the configuration cache, it
visits the reports container's internal pending map. If any reports were
registered lazily via reports.register(), the serializer encounters
ProviderInternal objects in that map; realizing those providers moves
entries out of the map while the serializer is still iterating it,
causing a ConcurrentModificationException.

Fix: after executing the user's configuration action inside reports{},
call reports.toList() to force-realize every pending item. This empties
the pending map before the configuration-cache serializer ever visits it,
so the ConcurrentModificationException no longer occurs.

Fixes #1539

Route Oqtane's internal IMemoryCache through FusionCache L1 so the
CMS benefits from the same L1+L2 caching stack as Portal code.

AddOqtane() in Program.cs. AddMemoryCache() uses TryAddSingleton so
our registration wins. FusionCache .WithRegisteredMemoryCache() now
adopts the same IMemoryCache singleton, making FusionCache L1 and

* Add Bicep infrastructure templates for Azure migration

Modular Bicep templates to deploy the full Agora CMS stack to Azure:

- networking.bicep: VNet with subnets for Container Apps + PostgreSQL,
  private DNS zone for Postgres
- postgres.bicep: Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server (B1ms),

  workspace) and Blob containers (originals + variants for device delivery)
- acr.bicep: Azure Container Registry (Basic tier)
- keyVault.bicep: Key Vault with RBAC authorization for secrets management
- containerApps.bicep: Container Apps Environment with CMS app (WebSocket
  ingress, Azure Files mount, 1 vCPU/2GB) and MCP server (0.25 vCPU/0.5GB)
- main.bicep: Orchestrator wiring all modules together
- prod.bicepparam: Production parameter template

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add storage abstraction layer for local/Azure backends

Implement StorageBackend ABC with two implementations:
- LocalStorageBackend: uses local filesystem (no-op sync hooks, FileResponse)
- AzureStorageBackend: syncs files to Azure Blob Storage after writes/deletes,
  serves device downloads via time-limited SAS URLs

Integration points:
- config.py: AGORA_CMS_STORAGE_BACKEND (local|azure) + Azure credentials
- main.py: initializes backend at startup based on config
- routers/assets.py: cloud sync on upload/delete, storage-aware downloads
- routers/ws.py: device download URLs use SAS URLs in Azure mode
- services/transcoder.py: syncs completed variants to Blob Storage

The local filesystem path (Docker volume or Azure Files mount) remains the
primary I/O layer for FFmpeg, checksums, and probing. The Azure backend
adds cloud sync + SAS URL generation on top.

New GraphQL mutations:

- updateUser (profile updates: bio, privacy, hide_days_played)
- resetApiToken (generate new API token)

- All HTML-only controllers (home, activity, search, settings, admin, etc.)
- Frontend assets (JS, CSS/SCSS, SVG icons, images)
- Webpack config, package.json, yarn.lock, tsconfig.json
- View helpers (application, activity, games, users, settings)
- Gems: propshaft, jsbundling-rails, turbolinks, jbuilder, inline_svg, invisible_captcha, web-console, launchy, capybara, selenium-webdriver

Updated tests:
- Removed feature specs (Capybara/Selenium - will be replaced by Playwright in frontend)
- Removed request specs for deleted HTML controllers
- Updated graphql_spec.rb for new auth flow (unauthenticated access + JWT)

* Fix authentication bypass in GraphQL API token auth

The api_user method returned a user by email alone without verifying
the API token. The token validation result was stored but never checked,
allowing any request with just a valid X-User-Email header to
authenticate as that user.

Now requires both X-User-Email and X-User-Token headers, and only sets
@api_token_user when verify_api_token! returns true.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Apnz6AoAeGn5bZpMPJSQK7

* Fix high-severity frontend issues from code review

- auth store: Wrap JSON.parse of localStorage in try/catch to prevent
  app crash from corrupted data
- GameShowPage: Add null-safe access to game.value?.name in async
  mutation callbacks, fix error casting
- UserShowPage: Add try/catch to follow/unfollow handlers, add
  :disabled binding to prevent double-click race conditions

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Apnz6AoAeGn5bZpMPJSQK7

* Require FRONTEND_URL env var in production for CORS

Previously fell back to http://localhost:5173 if FRONTEND_URL was unset,
which would be insecure in production. Now raises KeyError on boot if
the env var is missing in production.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Apnz6AoAeGn5bZpMPJSQK7

* Reject banned users in JWT and API token auth paths

Previously, banned users could continue executing GraphQL mutations
via JWT or API token authentication because the ban check in
sign_out_banned_users only applies to Devise session-based auth.
Now both auth paths check user.banned? before setting the
authenticated user, treating banned users as unauthenticated.

commit.message:
Harden allocation algorithm against SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED contention (#9)

* Initial plan

* feat: retry allocation processor on SELECT FOR UPDATE lock contention

When findByIdsForUpdate returns fewer locked rows than expected (SKIP
LOCKED skipped rows held by concurrent transactions), the processor now
retries up to MAX_RETRIES=3 times with a 50ms back-off instead of
immediately marking the allocation as FAILED.

Key logic:
- hasContention = lockedCandidates.size < candidateIds.size
- Contention → retry; no contention but algorithm fails → fail immediately
- Phase 1 (findByState) is re-executed each attempt to reflect equipment
  committed by concurrent transactions (PostgreSQL READ COMMITTED)

Also updates AllocationProcessorTest:
- Replaces old single-attempt contention test with a MAX_RETRIES test
- Adds test for successful allocation on retry (contention resolves)
- Adds test for immediate failure without retrying on genuine shortage
